def: s.m. An owl; — a turtle-dove; — (met.) a blockhead.

def: s.f. Melting, dissolving; fusion; softness, mellowness; mildness; tenderness (e.g. ghulāwaṭ-kī āṅkh, 'an eye (or glance) of tenderness').
s.f. Melting, dissolving; fusion; liquefaction.
v.t. To melt, dissolve; to mix (in or with, -meṅ); to cause to waste away; — (dialec.) to coax, wheedle, allure, entice, seduce, deceive.
more: Rus. v. a. Caus. of گهلنا q. v.

def: adj. (dakh.) Alluring, deceitful.

n. f. 1. ( ghulan ) Melting; fusion. 2. ( Poet .) A soft or tender look. Āṅkh ek ek par ghulāvaṭ kī, bāt ek ek se lagāvaṭ kī . Shauq. A tender look to each she throws, A winning word for each she knows. 3. Softness; mildness. Aur jis pe surkh joṛā yā ūdī oṛhnī hai. Us par to sab ghulāvaṭ barsāt kī chhanī hai . Nazīr. Some were in red, and some in purple robes bedight, Autumn's soft radiance mellowed seemed on them to light.
s.f. Melting, dissolving; fusion; softness, mellowness; mildness; tenderness (e.g. ghulāwaṭ-kī āṅkh, 'an eye (or glance) of tenderness').
s. (dakh.) Outcry, uproar, cry, din, clamour, disturbance, brawl, noise ( Harris ): see غل غپاره g̠ẖul-g̠ẖapāra under غل g̠ẖul.
s. m. 1. Noise, tumult. 2. A kind of dish.
adj. Dissolved; mixed; melted (in kindness); — in close union, intimate ( = ghulā-milā, q.v.s.v. ghulā)
subs: To be on very intimate terms; to live in close union or in harmony.

def: v.n. To be or become dissolved; to dissolve, melt; to be mixed (with, -meṅ), be blended or united (with); — to become soft, or mellow, or ripe (as fruit, &c.); to rot; — to become lean, to waste away (from grief or sickness); — to wrestle
more: v. n. 1. To melt; dissolve; to be dissolved; to thaw. Gāṅṭheṅ ghul gāiṅ . 2. To be ripe, mellow, softened (as fruit). 3. To be mingled, blended. Namak ghulnā . 4. To dwindle; waste; wear away (from grief or sickness). Bachchā bīmārī meṅ ghul gayā . 5. To pass or melt away. 6. To wrestle; fight. ghul mil jānā , v. n. To become very intimate. ghul mil-kar rahnā , v. n. To be very intimate. ghulā milā . Intimate; close; thick.
v. n. To melt, dissolve, be dissolved. 2. To be mellowed, to rot (as, fruit). 3. To become lean, to waste. گهل مل ghul-mil, part. Dissolved, mixed, melted (in kindness).

def: v.t. To cause to be melted or dissolved; — to cause to melt or dissolve; to cause to mix (with a liquid).
more: v. a. Caus. of گهولنا q. v.
v. a. To cause to mix with a liquid, to cause to dissolve.

def: s.m. Running round a pole (a game amongst children); — adv. Round, about; back.
more: n. S. घर्ण. Running round a pole. (Children). ghūm ghumānā , v. a. To make one dance attendance; to put one off from time to time.
s. (dakh.) A game amongst children: a stick is buried in the earth, with a string previously fastened to it, by holding which they run in a circle ( Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. lv).

ety: caus. of ghūmnā; — ghumā˚ = ghumāw˚ = Prk. घुम्मावे˚ = S. घूर्ण+आपि, caus. augment
def: v.t. To cause to turn or revolve; to turn round, whirl round; to wheel; to roll; to brandish, flourish; — to give (an animal, &c.) a turn round; to take round; to circulate; to encircle; — to lead astray; to beguile, delude
more: v. a. Caus. of ghūmnā , q. v. Sipāhīṛā! sīdhe chalo, nahīṅ mārūṅgī nainā ghumāë! Wom. Song. Soldier, go your way! or if my eyes on you I turn I'll slay you straight.
(caus. of گهومنا ghūmnā, q. v.) v. a. 1. To turn round, whirl round, wheel, roll, brandish, circulate, encircle. 2. To beguile, to delude. گهمانا پهرانا ghumānā phirānā, To prevaricate.
subs: v.t. To lead (one) a pretty round; — to prevaricate

def: v.n. To gather (as clouds).
v. n. To gather (clouds).
s.m. prop. ghumāʼo, q.v.

more: n. m. As much land as can be ploughed by one pair of bullocks in a day.

v.t. To lead (one) round about; to make (one) dance attendance; to practise evasion or procrastination with, to put (one) off from time to time.
(see گهمگهما) v. a. 1. To revolve. 2. To prevaricate.

def: v.n. To turn, turn round, revolve, whirl, spin (as a top, &c.), wheel, circle; to go round, to go round and round, to swim (as the head, e.g. sir ghūmnā); to be or become giddy or dizzy; — s.m. Turning round, whirling, &c.
more: v. n. 1. To turn round; revolve ( phirnā 1—3). Apne nainā mujhe de, tū ghūm phir-ke dekh! Prov. Give me your eyes, then turn about and see! (Said to a selfish person who would even ask you to give him the coat off your back). ghūmte tāre, chalte tāre , ( Ast .) The planets. ghūmtī lakīr (revolving line); A. nisf qutr (half chord); S. व्यासार्द्ध ( Geom .) A radius. ghūmghumā'lā, ghūmghumelā , adj. Full, wide (skirt, robe); having great girth; loose.
v. n. To go round, revolve, turn, roll, wheel, whirl; subst. Whirling, &c. سرگهومنا sir ghūmnā, To be giddy or dizzy, to swim (the head).

def: s.m. Rancour, &c. ( = ghun, q.v.). s.m. A kind of insect (destructive to wood, grain, and flour); wood-louse; weevil; — the dust or powder of worm-eaten wood or grain; — the pangolin or armadillo; — (met.) grief, sorrow; canker; — any wasting disease
subs: (-meṅ), To be attacked by the wood-louse or the weevil; to be worm-eaten; to be the prey of a wasting disease.

def: s.m. A kind of insect (destructive to wood, grain, and flour); wood-louse; weevil; — the dust or powder of worm-eaten wood or grain; — the pangolin or armadillo; — (met.) grief, sorrow; canker; — any wasting disease
more: n. m. S. घुण 1. (Sant. hutī ) A weevil. 2. The dust or powder of decayed wood or grain. 3. ( Met .) Grief; sorrow. ghun'nā, ghun lagnā, ghun jānā , v. n. 1. To be eaten or destroyed (as wood). 1. Khetvā meṅ ghunelā rahrī ke khuṅṭiyā, charūī meṅ ghunelā pisān, Choliyā meṅ ghune lā bālā re jobanā, mūrakh piyā agiyān . Bhoj. song. Pulse stumps are worm-eaten in fields, and meal stored in the house, so in my boddice wear my breasts, a novice yet my spouse. 2. Yeh umr jise tum samjhe ho yeh har dam tan ko chuntī hai, Jis lakṛī ke bal baiṭhe ho din rāt yeh lakṛī ghuntī hai . Nazīr. This life on which you count consumes you bit by bit, Each moment eaten is the branch on which you sit. 2. To be the prey of consuming disease. Bhalī chaṅgī thī maiṅ, ghun lag gayā, haë haë! javānī meṅ, Nigoṛe chīre-vāle, nauj khāūṅ maiṅ davā terī! Wom. Rāhat. In blooming health the canker worm infects my youth, Nev…
subs: (-meṅ), To be attacked by the wood-louse or the weevil; to be worm-eaten; to be the prey of a wasting disease.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit घिणा (ghiṇā), from Sanskrit घृणा (ghṛṇā).

The prepuce or foreskin ( galāf , 3). ghūṅgaṭ kāṛhnā, k., mārnā, yā nikālnā , v. a. To veil the face. Kab lag baiṭhī rahūṅ kāṛhe ghuṅgaṭvā, Mele tamāshe meṅ jāë nahīṅ deto! Oudh Punch. How long close-veiled must I remain? from fairs and shows my steps you e'er restrain! ghūṅgaṭ k . To arch the neck (a horse). ghūṅgaṭ-vālī , n. f. A veiled woman who does not appear before strange men. 1. Aë haryālī! ghūṅgaṭ khol! ghūṅgaṭ-vālī, ghūṅgaṭ khol! Marriage Song. Fair bride, lift up your veil! veiled one, your veil lift up. 2. Lambe ghūṅgaṭ-vālī se ḍarye! Prov. Saying. The close-veiled ones e'er fear!

A temporary fire place. He! ekar māë phuharī haik, teheṅ se ghūrā meṅ iaṛikā paklik heā . Mag. Ho ho! what a fool the mother must be to have roasted her child in the fire. (She fell asleep and let it drop).

n. m. S. घोटक, Pr. ghoḍao . 1. A horse. 1. Ghoṛoṅ ko ghar kitnī dūr? Prov. The horse's stable is not far. (He runs at a gallop). 2. Ghoṛe kī dum baṛhegī to apnī hī makkhī uṛāvegā . Prov. The horse's tail grown long he drives the flies that on him throng. (Self-advancement self promotes). 3. Kāṭh ke ghoṛā, adālat ke māmilā chalaulā se chalelā . Bhoj. Prov. A wooden horse and a suit-at-law must be driven ere an inch 'twill draw. 4. Sadāṅ miyāṅ ke hāṅ ghoṛe hī to rahe haiṅ! ( Ironic .) He has always had a stable full of horses! 2. The knight in chess. 3. The hammer of a gun. ghoṛā chaṛhānā , v. a. To cock a gun. ghoṛā dauṛānā , v. a. To make a horse gallop. ghoṛā ḍālnā , v. n. To give a horse his head. ghoṛe par jānā , v. n. To ride. ghoṛe par chaṛhnā yā savār h . To mount a horse. ghoṛe-savār , n. m. A horseman; a rider. ghoṛe kī shādī k. , (Horsedealers) To sell a horse. ☞ Shādī k . (to m…
s. m. 1. A horse. گهوڙي کو سرپٿ پهينکنا ghoṛe ko sarpaṭ phenknā, To force a horse to his utmost speed. 2. The cock (of a gun). گهوڙا چڙهانا ghoṛā chaṛhānā, To cock a gun.

....گُڑیا رے گُڑیا تیرا گُڈّا پَردیسِیا جوڑی آسمانی ہوگی شَگُن پِہ دیکھو شادمانی ہوگی ...

To throw heaps of earth on the buried corpse, esteemed a very pious act.

s. f. Earth, clay, mud. گل ارمني gil-i-armanī, s. f. Armenian bole. گل اندازي gil-andāz-ī, s. f. Imbankment. گل حکمت gil-i-ḥikmat, s. f. Lute, cement. گل در گل کرنا gil dar gil karnā, (lit.) To throw earth on earth (said when, in burying the dead, earth is thrown immediately on the corpse; whereas, the usual custom is, after depositing it in the grave, to lay planks over it and then fill up the remaining cavity with earth.

def: n. f. S. कालका 1. A squirrel. 1. Gilahrī kā peṛ ṭhikānā . Prov. The squirrel's home is on the tree. 2. Khedī gillī ant ko peṛ nīche ātī hai . Prov. The hunted squirrel runs to the tree. (The wanderer returns home at last). 3. Tumhārī gillo peṛā māṅgtī hai! Double entendre. 4. Karūṅgī dhūm se shādī, buā! nisbat to ṭhairī hai, Gilahrā hai merā, aur maṅjhlī bhābī kī gilahrī hai . Wom. Maiṅ itnā, maiṅ kitnā, merā dil bichārā kitnā, Piyāre-pīṭe ke hāth lagāyā, gul machāyā kitnā! Riddle. Ans. Bichchhā a scorpion. I'm such a tiny little thing, my heart so small, I laid my hand upon my love, how she did squall!

(contract. of غلغل) s. m. f. Noise, din, outcry, tumult. غل غپاره g̠ẖul-g̠ẖapāra, s. m. Clamour, disturbance, brawl. غل غدر g̠ẖul g̠ẖadr, Noise and tumult. غل مچانا g̠ẖul machānā, To make a noise, outcry or tumult, to hoot.
s. m. Hatred, malice, perfidy, fraud, trick, envy. غل وغش g̠ẖill-o-g̠ẖish, s. f. Apprehension, objection, fraud, deceit. بي غل و غش be g̠ẖill-o-g̠ẖish, Without hesitation, frankly, boldly.
subs: s.m. Fraud, deceit; — apprehension; objection, hesitation
adv. Without guile; without hesitation, frankly, boldly.

An affix to nouns, signifying full of, affected with, possessed of, as surma-gīṅ full of surmā, gam-gīn sorrowful.

ety: contrac. of āgīn, rt. of āgandan; — prep. ā+Zend rt. kan = S. खन्
def: A suffix (denoting 'full of, or affected with, or possessed of') added to nouns to form possessive adjectives (e.g. surma-gīn, 'full of surma'; g̠am-gīn, 'sorrowful, sad').
more: (a particle which, added to nouns, denotes) Full of, affected with; as, غمگين g̠ẖam-gīn, Sorrowful; سرمه گين surma-gīn, Full of surma, &c.

ety: perf. part. of ginnā, &c.; — and = Prk. गणिअओ; S. गणित+कः, rt. गण्
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Numbered, counted, reckoned, &c. (see ginnā)
subs: (or napā) shorbā, prov. 'Counted (or a limited number of) lumps of meat (necessitate) a measured supply of soup' (applied to one who is exact in his expenses to a degree of penury; and to one whose income is hardly equal to his expenditure); — living from hand to mouth; — cut your coat according to your cloth.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

Say 'cock-a-doodle do,' sweet cakes I'll give to you, Talk to me nicely, do, nice sugar plums for you, Cry, baby, if you do, and you shall have thumps two.

ety: gin˚ or gaṇ˚ = Prk. गण(इ) = S. गणय(ति), rt. गण्
def: v.t. To count, reckon, number, calculate, compute; to consider, deem, repute, esteem
more: v. a. S. गुणन 1. ( gintī k .) To count; calculate ( shumār k .). 1. Gin gin-ke gāliyāṅ dī haiṅ . Wom. She abused all my relations in succession. 2. Gine na saṅbhāle, ṭhā ṭhā galle ghāle! Rus. May you never need to count nor take stock, but in your cash-box money constant drop! (May God so enrich you!). 3. Gin poīṅ, saṅbhāl khāīṅ . Prov. She counts each cake, and sparingly she eats. 4. Ripu, ruj, pāvak, pāp, prabhu, ahī, gaṅṛye na chhoṭ kari! As kahi bibidh bilāp kari lāgi rodan karan . Rāmāyan. A foe, disease, fire, sin, a snake account not slight! So saying with loud shrieks and cries she wept outright. 2. To take into account; regard. 1. Garīb ko kaun gintā hai ? No one cares for the poor. 2. Man māne jab kare laṛāi, taṛkā gine na sānjh , (A Slut). Sikhar du-pahrī pīsan baiṭhī ghāl chhāj meṅ nāj . To fight too ready all, nor evening heeds nor morn, At noon her basket filled she sits to…
v. a. To count, reckon, number, calculate, compute.
subs: To count one's steps; — to move very slowly or with great caution
or ginne-lāʼiq, adj. To be counted, calculable, numerable.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

ety: Prk. गणत्तिआ; S. गण+आप्तिकां (i.e. आपि caus. aug.+ति+का
def: s.f. Counting, computing, calculating, reckoning; — calculation; enumeration; number; addition; the rule of addition (in Arith.); muster; muster-roll; — the first, or the last, day (of a month); — (fig.) reckoning, account, consideration, esteem (syn. shumār); care; value (for)
more: n. f. S. गणित 1. See shumār . 1. Tum kis gintī meṅ ho? Of what account are you? 2. Sau lo gintī, pāoṅ lo bintī . Prov. It sufficeth in counting a hundred to reach, or to fall at the feet of him you beseech. 2. The first and last days of the month. 3. A muster-roll. gintī ke , adj. Few ( chand 1). Gintī ke log the. gintī ginvānā , v. a. To make a false return. gīnti lenā , v. a. To have anything counted; to muster. gun-gintī-zanjīr , Geometrical progression.
s. f. 1. Number, reckoning, account. 2. Muster.
subs: adj. That can (or could) be counted; a small number, few (e.g. gintī-ke log the)
(-kī), To make a false roll or return (of)
(-kī), To take an account (of); to have (things) counted; to number; to muster
wety: From گننا (ginnā, “to count”).

def: v.t. To cause to be counted, to cause to be reckoned up; to have (or get) counted, &c.
more: v. a. Caus. of گنا q. v.
v. a. (causal of گنا ginnā ) To cause to count.

s. m. A sheep or goatʼs ventricle stuffed with minced meat and rice, a pudding.

def: s.m. A seller of puddings.

ety: rt. of giriftan, fr. Zend garew = S. ग्रम्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Taking, seizing, holding; conquering; — taker, holder, catcher, conqueror, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ʻālam-gīr, 'conqueror of the world'; — gul-gīr, 'snuffers')
more: used in Comp. only. Taking; seizing; holding; taker; holder; conqueror, etc. as Gul-gīr snuffers; Ālam-gīr , conqueror of the world.

1. part. (of گرفتن) in compos. Taking, seizing, holding, taker, holder, catcher, conquerour, &c. s. Taking, holding. 2. imper. 2d pers. sing. Take, seize. گير و دار gīr-o-dār, Take and hold, taking and holding; absolute sway or rule.
subs: or gīr-dār, s.m. lit. 'Take and hold'; taking and holding; dominion, absolute sway or rule; — battle, contest; confused clamour of combatants.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گیر (gīr, “a captor, taker, seizer”).

def: Names of Kr̤ish̤ṇa.

def: adj. & s.m. Taking, seizing; — prehensile; — treating, handling; — taker, seizer; holder; — one who is held firmly; a captive; — detention; captivity (used as last member of compounds, e.g. mīrās̤-gīrā, 'one who takes an inheritance, the heir-at-law to an estate').
more: (part. act. of گرفتن) adj. Taking, taker.

s.f. Taking, seizing, holding; grasp, seizure; the power of taking or laying hold; prehension, prehensibility; — (in India) the Ṭhagī Department.

def: adj. Revered, respected; excellent; great; dear, beloved, precious.
more: (improp. garāmī ) adj. Dear, precious, excellent, revered, great.

ety: fr. Zend gara, rt. gar = S. gal; see garāmī
def: adj. Heavy, weighty, ponderous; great, important, momentous; difficult; burdensome, grievous; — precious, valuable; dear, expensive
more: (improp. garān ) adj. 1. Heavy, important, momentous. 2. Precious, dear. گرانبار girān-bār, adj. Heavily laden, fruit-bearing, pregnant, wealthy. گران خاطر girān-ḵẖat̤ir, adj. Sad, dejected. گران مايه girān-māya, adj. Of great value, valuable.
s. (dakh.) Eclipse ( Harris ): see گرهن grahaṇ.
subs: adj. Heavily-laden; fruit-bearing; pregnant (woman or animal); wealthy; grave, serious; deliberate, cool
s.f. Pregnancy; gravity, steadiness; deliberation, coolness
adj. Heavy on, or burdensome to, the heart; unpleasant; afflicted, sad, dejected
v.t. To make heavy or burdensome; to raise or enhance (the value or price of)

adj. Weighty, ponderous; precious, of great value, valuable; of noble birth or stock
v.n. To be burdensome, or grievous; to be or become dear, to rise (in value or price).

def: v.t. To cause to fall, to fell; to cast down, throw down; to break down; to overthrow, overturn, upset; to let fall, to drop; — to bring low, reduce, ruin, destroy; to spill, shed (blood, &c.); to strike
more: v. a. S. गल to fall down. 1. See girnā , v. n. 2. To let fall; drop; spill; shed.

(caus. of گرنا) v. a. 1. To cause to fall, to fell, to overturn or overthrow, to abase, to cast, throw or break down. 2. To spill, to drop, to shed. 3. To strike. گرادينا girā denā, To throw, beat, cast or break down.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = grantham, q.v. s.m. A character in which Sanskrit is written and printed in Southern India (where the Devanāgarī character is unknown).

s.f. Weight, burden; heaviness; gravity; importance; — scarceness, scarcity, dearth, dearness; rise (in price); — heaviness of spirit; depression; grief, vexation; — indigestion; a disease happening to women in parturition.
more: n. f. 1. Dearness; scarcity; dearth; a rise in price. Galle kī garānī hai qayāmat kī nishānī, paṛtā nahīṅ pānī, Sarkār ne aur us pe hai yeh ṭeks jamāyā, faryād Khudāyā! Oudh Punch. With grain so dear the judgment must be near, no rain doth fall, And now a tax to pay withal—God save thy people all! 2. Heaviness of spirits ( udāsī ). 3. Indigestion. Pet meṅ garānī hai . 4. A disease happening to women in parturition.
s. f. 1. Scarcity, dearth, dearness. 2. Weight, heaviness, importance.
